source: NvChad - Install Neovim: why EPEL is discouraged, the precompiled install, verification and the symlink

questions.q: Why is installing Neovim from EPEL discouraged for NvChad?
options: The EPEL version is too old to meet NvChad's minimum requirements
It installs Vim instead of Neovim
questions.why: EPEL's Neovim is too old; the precompiled package or source is recommended.

questions.q: Which precompiled archive do you download for a 64-bit Linux install?
questions.why: The linux64 build ships as nvim-linux64.tar.gz (plus its .sha256sum).

questions.q: What command verifies the download against its checksum file?
questions.why: sha256sum -c reads the .sha256sum file and reports OK on a good download.

questions.q: In this guide, where is the precompiled package unpacked?
questions.why: tar xvzf ... -C ~/.local/share/ (the location is up to you).

questions.q: After unpacking, what makes nvim runnable from your PATH?
options: A symbolic link to the nvim executable placed in ~/.local/bin/

questions.q: What command prints the installed Neovim version?
questions.why: nvim -v shows e.g. NVIM v0.9.5 and the LuaJIT version.

questions.q: A basic Neovim install, on its own, is...
options: a good editor but not yet an IDE until configured (e.g. with NvChad)
questions.why: The base editor is fine but needs configuration to become an IDE.
